title: Voyager: An Open-Ended Embodied Agent with Large Language Models
hook: Voyager lets an agent invent, remember, and reuse skills as it survives in Minecraft.
promise: You will see how Voyager enables agents to autonomously learn and reuse skills in complex worlds.
prerequisite_note: Bring a sense for how agents can take actions and learn from feedback in a virtual world.

glossary.plain: A virtual or physical entity that acts in the world and senses the results.
A neural network trained on lots of text, able to generate and reason about language.
A collection of reusable code snippets or routines learned by the agent to solve tasks.
A process where agents continually discover new abilities without a fixed end goal.
Automatically creating a sequence of challenges that gradually increase in difficulty.
Abilities or routines created autonomously by the agent, not hand-coded by humans.

cards.title: An Agent That Writes Its Own Playbook
body: Voyager creates and reuses its own skills to solve new problems in Minecraft.

cards.title: Can an agent learn like a person?
body: Imagine playing Minecraft for the first time. You learn to punch trees, craft tools, and build shelter, inventing useful tricks as you go.
Voyager tries to give AI agents this kind of open-ended learning: not just following instructions, but inventing and remembering skills to tackle new situations.

cards.title: How Voyager discovers and reuses skills
body: Voyager starts as a blank slate, facing the vast world of Minecraft with no built-in skills.
Each time Voyager solves a problem, it writes new code for that skill and saves it in a growing library, so it can reuse and combine skills later.
cards.caption: Voyager builds up a toolbox of its own skills, unlocking harder challenges.

cards.title: Handcrafted skills vs. Voyager's skill discovery
body: Toggle to compare agents with fixed skills against Voyager's self-invented approach.
cards.offNote: Agent relies on a fixed set of human-coded actions and cannot adapt to new tasks.
cards.onNote: Agent invents new skills as needed and reuses them for unexpected challenges.

cards.title: Let the agent write its own code
body: Instead of giving the agent a limited set of actions, Voyager lets it write Python code to invent new skills when it encounters a novel task.
These skills are stored in a library, so Voyager can string them together or adapt them for bigger problems, just like building with Lego bricks.

cards.title: How much skill reuse is enough?
body: Move the slider to see what happens as the agent saves and reuses more or fewer skills.
cards.low: Agent must solve each problem from scratch, learning is slow.
cards.mid: Agent reuses some skills, moderate learning speed and flexibility.
cards.high: Agent reuses many skills, learns rapidly and tackles harder challenges.

cards.title: What ingredients make Voyager work?
body: Select the core ingredients that enable Voyager to invent and reuse skills in Minecraft.

choices.label: Large Language Model
choices.note: Generates and revises Python code for new skills.

choices.label: Skill Library
choices.note: Stores reusable code snippets for future use.

choices.label: Automatic Evaluation
choices.note: Checks if a new skill works as intended in the game.

choices.label: Curriculum Generation
choices.note: Creates a sequence of challenges that guide skill discovery.

choices.label: Embodied Minecraft Agent
choices.note: Acts in the world and observes results, like a player.

choices.label: Self-Reflection Loop
choices.note: Agent reviews past actions to improve or invent new skills.

cards.title: Voyager outpaces other agents in Minecraft
body: Voyager solved more unique tasks and survived longer than previous agents, showing the power of open-ended skill creation.
Its skill library grew over time, allowing it to tackle increasingly complex challenges that other agents could not handle.

bars.label: Unique Skills Learned
bars.note: Voyager invents and remembers more skills

bars.label: Survival Time
bars.note: Agent survives longer in the game

cards.caption: Voyager's self-invented skills give it a clear edge in open-ended environments.

cards.title: Why Voyager matters for AI
body: Voyager shows that agents can go beyond human-coded routines, inventing and reusing their own skills to thrive in complex worlds.
This approach hints at a future where AI can autonomously grow its abilities, adapting to new tasks without step-by-step programming.

recap.label: Observe
recap.note: Agent faces a new problem in Minecraft.

recap.label: Invent
recap.note: Writes code for a new skill using a language model.

recap.label: Store
recap.note: Saves useful skills in a growing library.

recap.label: Reuse
recap.note: Applies old skills to solve harder tasks.

cards.title: Voyager: An Open-Ended Embodied Agent with Large Language Models
body: Explore the full paper for technical details, experiments, and more insights into Voyager's design.
